ShortDescription.Value: Supply commercial shops from your own city warehouses instead of every shop importing on its own.

Publish.LongDescription:
Turns your warehouses and cargo yards into a real distribution layer. It unlocks every physical, tradable good for storage (food, furniture, electronics and the rest — you still choose per building what each warehouse stocks), then supplies commercial shops from that local stock instead of each shop driving out to industry or importing on its own.
Master switch: - ON: shops are supplied from city warehouses (push). Warehouses import in bulk; individual shops don't. No warehouse capacity for a needed good means shops run short until you build it ("no building, no service"). - OFF (default per building): pure vanilla pull — shops fall back to buying from industry / imports.
Warehouse-owned delivery trucks now physically haul goods from your warehouses to the shops that need them, using the game's own delivery system. Alpha software: reverting the storage unlock takes effect after a game restart.
Source (open source, MIT): https://github.com/AmicusDeus/WholesaleLogistics
Made with Claude Code's Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 models.

ChangeLog.Value: Added an open-source GitHub link and a 'Made with Claude Code' note to the description.
